c74object.name: void.linkaudio.receive~
c74object.digest: Receive audio from an Ableton Link Audio channel and expose Link timing as audio-rate signals
c74object.description: void.linkaudio.receive~ subscribes to an audio channel published on the local Ableton Link Audio network and outputs the received signal on its left and right outlets. In addition, it exposes the current Link session timing as three audio-rate signals (tempo, phase, transport) sampled at the start of every DSP buffer, which makes them suitable for sample-accurate scheduling, modulation, and triggering downstream. It works seamlessly with Ableton Live, TouchDesigner, VCV Rack, and any other Link Audio host on the same LAN. Subscription is automatic and resilient: when the polling clock is enabled, the object continually retries to match the requested channel against currently advertised channels, transparently reconnecting after peer drops or channel renaming. Built on top of Ableton Link Audio (GPL-2.0-or-later, by Ableton AG); not endorsed by Ableton.

inlet.description: Accepts messages such as bang and info, plus attribute setters like poll, fromchannel, frompeer, tempo, transport, etc.

outlet.description: Left channel of the received audio stream. For mono streams, this carries the entire signal (and is also mirrored on the right outlet). Outputs silence when no subscription is active.
Right channel of the received audio stream. For mono streams, mirrors the left outlet. Outputs silence when no subscription is active.

Current Link session tempo in BPM, emitted as an audio-rate signal. Slowly varying. Updated at the start of every DSP buffer from captureAudioSessionState(). Independent of subscription state: this outlet keeps producing meaningful values even when no audio channel is currently being received.

Sawtooth phase signal in the range [0, quantum), derived from beatAtTime() % quantum at the start of every DSP buffer and advanced linearly across the buffer using beatsPerSample = tempo / 60 / sampleRate. Wraps cleanly at quantum boundaries. Useful for downbeat detection (wrap detector), phase-locked LFOs, and quantized triggering.

Mirrors the Link session's transport state as a binary audio-rate signal: 1.0 when the session is playing, 0.0 otherwise. Follows the result of session.isPlaying(). Requires startStopSync on the session, which is enabled internally.

Status output. A dictionary <name> message is emitted automatically whenever a meaningful state change occurs (subscription gained/lost, peer count changed, channel list changed) and on demand via the info message. Route through dict.view, dict.unpack, or route to consume it.

method.name: bang
method.description: Re-applies the current state. If a matching channel is now available on the network and the object is not yet subscribed, this triggers an immediate subscription attempt. State changes resulting from the retry will produce an automatic dict dump on the dumpout. This message produces no output by itself.

method.name: info
method.description: Outputs a structured dictionary on the dumpout containing the full current state: enabled, audio_enabled, num_peers, subscribed, fromchannel, frompeer, stream_sample_rate, stream_num_channels, frames_received, frames_dropped, tempo, transport, beat, phase, quantum, poll, pollinterval, num_audio_channels, plus a sub-dictionary channels listing every channel currently advertised on the network as index -> [channel_name, peer_name].

attribute.name: enable
attribute.description: When set to 1 (default), the object participates in the Link Audio network and attempts to subscribe to the requested channel. When set to 0, the object is fully detached: any active subscription is dropped and the audio outlets emit silence. Timing outlets keep producing valid values regardless of this setting, as long as the underlying Link session is alive.

attribute.name: frompeer
attribute.digest: From Peer (the source SEND's Peer Name)
attribute.description: Peer Name of the SEND we want to subscribe to (matches the value the source peer set in its own Peer Name parameter). Essential to disambiguate when several peers publish a channel with the same name. Without it, the first match wins. Empty by default; set it to the exact peer name announced by the source (e.g. VCVRack, Live, TouchDesigner).

attribute.name: fromchannel
attribute.digest: From Channel (channel name to subscribe to)
attribute.description: Exact name of the audio channel to receive on the source peer (matches the Channel Name parameter on the publisher side). Channel names are case-sensitive and must match exactly what the publishing peer advertises (e.g. Main for Live's master output). When this attribute changes, any active subscription is dropped and a new subscription is attempted at the next polling tick.

attribute.name: quantum
attribute.description: Number of beats per quantum, used by both the phase~ outlet (sawtooth period) and the beat / phase values reported via info. Range 1.0 to 16.0, default 4.0. Does not affect audio reception itself; purely a framing convenience for downstream beat-aware logic.

attribute.name: tempo
attribute.description: Read/write mirror of the Link session tempo. Reading queries the live session via captureAppSessionState().tempo(), so values reflect changes initiated by any peer (Live, another Max patch, etc.). Writing commits a new tempo back to the session, which propagates to all peers. Range 20.0 to 999.0 BPM. Does not produce attribute-modified messages on every external change; mirror sync to attrui / pattr happens via the polling clock (default 250 ms).

attribute.name: transport
attribute.description: Read/write mirror of the Link session's transport state (play/stop). Reading queries captureAppSessionState().isPlaying(); writing commits a new state via setIsPlaying(). Propagation between peers requires Start/Stop Sync on each side; this object enables it internally on the shared session. 1 = playing, 0 = stopped. As with tempo, mirror sync to attrui happens via the polling clock.

attribute.name: poll
attribute.description: When set to 1 (default), an internal Max clock fires every pollinterval milliseconds and (1) re-applies the object state for resilient subscription, (2) refreshes the local tempo and transport attribute storage from the live session and notifies observers (attrui, pattr) via object_attr_touch. Set to 0 to disable the clock; you can then drive retries manually with bang, but external tempo/transport changes will no longer auto-update the attributes.

attribute.name: pollinterval
attribute.description: Interval in milliseconds between automatic state-reapply ticks when poll is enabled. Range 50 to 60000, default 250. Lower values react faster to network and tempo/transport changes at a marginal CPU cost; higher values save CPU but slow reconnection and attribute mirror refresh.
